title: announce: encode_coords could produce out-of-range values with floating point edge cases
description:
File: python/src/lichen/announce/coords.py:59-66

The encode_coords function uses round() which can produce boundary values:

```python
lat_raw = int(round(lat * _SCALE))  # _SCALE = 10_000_000
lon_raw = int(round(lon * _SCALE))

if not (_LAT_E7_MIN <= lat_raw <= _LAT_E7_MAX):  # -900000000 to +900000000
    raise ValueError(...)
```

Floating point representation of 90.0 could have rounding issues. For example:
- If lat = 89.999999999 (just under 90)
- lat * _SCALE = 899999999.9999999... 
- round() = 900000000 (exactly max)
- This passes validation

But consider lat = 90.00000001 (just over 90):
- First check: _LAT_MIN <= 90.00000001 <= _LAT_MAX passes (90 <= 90.00000001 is True in first check??)

Actually wait, let me re-check:
```python
if not (_LAT_MIN <= lat <= _LAT_MAX):  # -90 <= lat <= 90
```

If lat = 90.00000001:
- -90 <= 90.00000001 is True
- 90.00000001 <= 90 is False  
- The combined check is False
- 'not False' = True, so we raise ValueError

The pre-rounding check catches this. This is actually correct. NOT a bug.

CANCELLING this bead.
